session.user:
In the file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/erk/replan.md`:
This file documents a CLI command. The project has renamed programmatic field names from "plan" to "pr" in the API/CLI. Update this file to match.
**Rename these patterns:** 1. `plan_number` → `pr_number` (variable names, JSON field names like `original_plan_number`, `new_plan_number`) 2. `plan_url` → `pr_url` 3. `plan-number` → `pr-number` (kebab-case in argument-hint and placeholders like `<plan-number-or-url>`) 4. `plan #<number>` → `PR #<number>` (output messages like "Created new plan #<new_number>" → "Created new PR #<new_number>") 5. In JSON examples like `"plan_number": 123` → `"pr_number": 123`
**DO NOT rename:** - "plan" as a concept noun (e.g., "replan", "plan content", "original plan") - `plan.md` file references - Skill/command names containing "plan" (e.g., `/erk:replan`) - `plan_backend` field name - "erk-plan" or "erk-consolidated" label names - Function/command names like `get-plan-metadata`, `update-plan-header`, `add-plan-labels`, `close-prs`, `get-plan-info`, `add-plan-label`
Read the file first, then apply all renames using the Edit tool.
session.assistant: I'll start by reading the file to understand its current content and structure.

tool_use.name: Read
tool_use.param: /home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/erk/replan.md

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: Replan an existing plan against current codebase state 3→argument-hint: <plan-number-or-url> 4→--- 5→ 6→# /erk:replan 7→ 8→Recomputes existing plan(s) against the current codebase state, creating a new plan and closing the original(s). 9→ 10→Supports consolidating multiple plans into a single unified plan. 11→ 12→## Usage 13→ 14→```bash 15→/erk:replan 2521 # Single plan replan 16→/erk:replan https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521 17→/erk:replan 123 456 789 # Consolidate multiple plans 18→``` 19→ 20→--- 21→ 22→## Agent Instructions 23→ 24→### Step 1: Parse Plan References 25→ 26→Split `$ARGUMENTS` on whitespace. For each argument: 27→ 28→- If numeric (e.g., `2521`), use directly as plan number 29→- If URL (e.g., `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/2521`), extract the number from the path 30→

Proceeding with replan anyway. 146→Return an error to the main agent: 149→Error: Plan #<number> not found. 481→## Error Cases 483→| Error | Message | 485→| Plan not found | `Error: Plan #<number> not found.` | 486→| Not a plan | `Error: Plan #<number> is not a valid plan (missing both erk-plan and erk-learn labels).` | 487→| No plan content | `Error: No plan content found in plan #<number>.` | 488→| GitHub CLI not available | `Error: GitHub CLI (gh) not available. Run: brew install gh && gh auth login` | 489→| No network | `Error: Unable to reach GitHub. Check network connectivity.` |

JSON field renames like `original_plan_number` → `original_pr_number`

session.assistant:
Excellent! All instances have been renamed. Let me create a summary of the changes made:
## Summary
I successfully updated the `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/erk/replan.md` file to rename programmatic field names from "plan" to "pr" terminology. Here are the changes applied:
### Renames Completed:
1. **Variable names (`plan_number` → `pr_number`):** - Line 3: `<plan-number-or-url>` → `<pr-number-or-url>` in argument-hint - Line 28: "as plan number" → "as pr number" - Line 31: "all plan numbers" → "all pr numbers" - Line 33: "plan number" → "pr number" - Line 44: "plan numbers" → "pr numbers" - Line 46: "plan numbers" → "PR numbers" - Line 297: `<original_plan_number>` → `<original_pr_number>` in template - Line 397: `<new_plan_number>` → `<new_pr_number>` - Line 401: `<new_plan_number>` → `<new_pr_number>` - Line 405: `"plan_number"` → `"pr_number"` in JSON example - Line 405: `<new_plan_number>` → `<new_pr_number>` - Line 413: `"pr_number"` in JSON - Line 419: `"pr_number"` fields in JSON examples - Line 456: `<new_plan_number>` → `<new_pr_number>` - Line 468: `<new_plan_number>` → `<new_pr_number>`
2. **Output messages (`plan #<number>` → `PR #<number>`):** - Line 89, 93: `Plan #<number>` → `PR #<number>` - Line 99: `Plan #<number>` → `PR #<number>` - Line 149: `Plan #<number>` → `PR #<number>` - Line 427: "Created new plan" → "Created new PR" - Line 428: "Closed original plan" → "Closed original PR" - Line 438: "Created consolidated plan" → "Created consolidated PR" - Line 439: "Closed original plans" → "Closed original PRs" - Line 474: `Plan #<number>` → `PR #<number>` - Line 485-487: Error messages updated to use `PR #<number>`
### Not Renamed (Correctly Preserved):
- "plan" as a concept noun (e.g., "Replan an existing plan", "plan content", "plan items") - `plan.md` file references - Skill/command names like `/erk:replan` and `/local:replan-learn-plans` - Label names: `erk-plan`, `erk-learn`, `erk-consolidated` - Function names: `get-plan-info`, `update-plan-header`, `add-plan-labels`, `close-prs`, `add-plan-label` - Field names: `plan_backend`, `objective_issue`
The file has been successfully updated with all the required terminology changes while preserving the appropriate use of "plan" as a conceptual term and in function/label names.
